mission.title: opentofu: Support the XDG Base Directory Specification
mission.description:
This PR introduces support for XDG environment variables. `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` for config and `XDG_DATA_HOME` for plugins.

If `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set and `~/.tofurc` or `~/.terraformrc` don't exist, `XDG_CONFIG_HOME/opentofu/tofurc` will be used as config file.
If `XDG_DATA_HOME` is set, plugins will be searched in `XDG_DATA_HOME/opentofu` as well as the default `~/.terraform.d`.

This ensures that existing config and plugins will still get used, but allow users to gradually migrate to the new structure, with and without setting `XDG_*` environment variables.

The CLI ensures that the intermediate config directory exists, so if `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set and the default `~/.terraform.d` directory doesn't exist `XDG_CONFIG_HOME/opentofu` is created.

Only affects unix* platforms.

Resolves #303

## Target Release

1.7.0

resolution.summary:
This PR introduces support for XDG environment variables. `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` for config and `XDG_DATA_HOME` for plugins.

If the `~/.terraform.d` directory doesn't exist, config is assumed to live in `XDG_CONFIG_HOME/opentofu` with `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` defaulting to `~/.config`.
`~/.local/share/opentofu` and `~/.terraform.d` are always used for looking up plugins with `XDG_DATA_HOME` as third option when it is set to a non-default value.

This ensures that existing config and plugins will still get used, but allow users to gradually migrate to the new structure, with and without setting `XDG_*` environment variables.

The CLI ensures that the intermediate config directory exists, so either `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/opentofu` or `~/.config/opentofu` will be created if neither `~/.terraformrc`, `~/.tofurc` or `~/.terraform.d` exist. The lookup behavior remains unchanged after this directory was created.

Only affects unix* platforms.

Resolves #303

## Target Release

1.7.0
steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request
